_meta.note: VA batch: Griffith (R-VA-09 federal), Subramanyam (D-VA-10 federal), Walkinshaw (D-VA-11 federal), Waverly Washington (WITHDREW from R-VA-07 primary — null all cells), Andrew Rice (R-VA HOD-98 state, sworn 2026-03-17 — thin record). profile.religion: Episcopalian (per Pew Research congressional affiliation tracking)
profile.background: U.S. House, VA-09, since 2011. Former VA Delegate. Chairs E&C Environment Subcommittee; E&C Health Subcommittee role. SW Virginia coal country. Heritage Action 74% lifetime.

morgan-griffith.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-09 (R), since 2011. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Documented conservative voting on marriage (NAY Respect for Marriage Act 2022), parents/schools (YEA Parents Bill of Rights; Mahmoud v. Taylor amicus), border (YEA H.R.2 Secure the Border), election integrity (YEA SAVE Act), 2A (NRA A+, GOA-endorsed; opposed Bipartisan Safer Communities Act on red-flag/due-process), life (SBA Pro-Life endorsed; Pain-Capable/Hyde votes). INDUSTRY-CAPTURE FLAG: Sludge (Dec 2024) identified Griffith as the top pharma campaign-cash recipient now chairing the E&C Health Subcommittee overseeing the industry — significant capture concern; not scored as a TRUE on 10.x. Foreign policy: no clean restraint signal found — all 9.x null. Christian liberty + euthanasia + IVF + porn age-verify + faith-based adoption: no public statements located — null per evidence-first rule.

2.note: Joined amicus brief in Mahmoud v. Taylor opposing forced gender-ideology curriculum in K-12.

4.note: Mahmoud amicus + Parents Bill of Rights — opposed LGBTQ promotion in public schools.

1.note: Voted YEA on H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights Act (2023) — parental notification/consent on curriculum and medical decisions.

2.note: Mahmoud v. Taylor amicus + Parents Bill of Rights — opposes CRT/SOGI/gender-ideology curriculum.

1.note: Voted YEA on the SAVE Act (H.R.8281, 2024) — proof-of-citizenship to register, voter ID + citizenship verification.

profile.religion: Hindu (engaged with BAPS Shri Swaminarayan Mandir, Chantilly VA; recognized Rajdhani Mandir 25th anniversary on House floor)
profile.background: U.S. House VA-10 since Jan 3, 2025. Former VA state senator (SD-32, 2024-2025) and delegate (HD-87, 2020-2024). Loudoun-based. First Indian American from VA in Congress. Heritage Action 119th score: 9%.

suhas-subramanyam-cd10.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-10 (D), since Jan 2025. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Progressive Democrat — documented FALSE on life (PP-endorsed, voted to shield abortion providers from extradition at VA-state level; opposed SAVE Act), marriage (LGBTQ Resource Guide on official site, opposed HB2432 parental-notification on gender transition), family (defends SOGI inclusion, opposed parental-notification), 2A (Giffords-endorsed, authored VA red-flag bills), election integrity (NAY on SAVE Act). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS (HIGH-VALUE): voted YES on Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025 — mandatory DHS detention for illegals arrested for theft/burglary, federally pre-empts sanctuary release); voted YES on ICC Sanctions Bill HR 23 (Jan 2025 — one of ~10 freshman Ds backing sanctions over Israel warrants); voted FOR Iran War Powers Resolution affirming Article I requirement. Religion: Hindu. Christian-liberty cells null — no specific anti-Christian-profession statement located; religious recognition of Hindu temples is symbolic not state-funded promotion under the rubric.

0.note: Voted (as VA legislator) to roll back 24-hr waiting period, ultrasound, and counseling — incompatible with life-from-conception affirmation.

1.note: Voted to shield abortion providers from extradition and disciplinary action; opposes restrictions — opposite of abolition advocacy.

2.note: Backed contraceptive/reproductive-rights framework that includes IVF expansion — does not oppose embryo discard.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2026 cycle (VA-10) and previously by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia — HARD evidence of PP-aligned support.

0.note: Self-described 'strong supporter of the LGBTQ+ community'; maintains LGBTQ Resource Guide on official House site — incompatible with man-woman marriage affirmation.

1.note: Vocal LGBTQ-rights backer — does not oppose same-sex marriage / civil unions.

2.note: Opposed VA HB2432 requiring parental notification of gender-incongruence counseling; publicly vowed to fight Trump-admin transgender policy.

4.note: Defended school-based trans protections in Loudoun and opposed restrictions — supports LGBTQ promotion in schools.

1.note: Voted against VA HB2432 — parental notification of gender-transition counseling.

2.note: Defends SOGI inclusion as protection of 'vulnerable' students — does not oppose SOGI curricula.

1.note: Voted against the SAVE Act (Feb 11, 2025) — opposed proof-of-citizenship to register.

CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YES on the Laken Riley Act (Jan 7, 2025) — mandatory DHS detention of illegal aliens arrested for theft/burglary/shoplifting; one of 48 House Ds in favor. Documented credit for the mandatory-detention precursor to deportation.

2.note: Laken Riley YES — federally pre-empts sanctuary release of illegals arrested for crimes; partial but documented opposition to sanctuary-style policy on this category.

0.note: Giffords-endorsed gun-control candidate; opposes constitutional carry.

1.note: Authored 'extreme risk' (red-flag) legislation as VA senator and expanded universal background checks — supports red-flag laws and gun restrictions.

2.note: Backs federal gun regulation; opposes NFA/GCA repeal.

4.note: Champions ATF-style regulation; introduced VA bill restricting firearms on school property.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY-ADJACENT DEVIATION: voted FOR the Iran War Powers Resolution affirming the Article I congressional war-powers requirement before military action against Iran.

profile.religion: Not publicly disclosed — no church/denomination on Ballotpedia or biographical sources
profile.background: U.S. House VA-11 since Sept 2025 (won special election after Rep. Gerry Connolly's May 21, 2025 death). Former Fairfax County Board of Supervisors (Braddock District) 2019-2025; Connolly's longtime chief of staff before that. Progressive Democrat.

james-walkinshaw.notes_set: U.S. Rep VA-11 (D), since Sept 2025 special election. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Strong Fairfax County BOS record (2019-2025) provides documented FALSEs on most cells: authored the 2021 Fairfax 'Trust Policy' ending voluntary ICE cooperation (sanctuary author — DHS publicly named him a 'Fairfax sanctuary politician' Dec 2025); championed Fairfax red-flag implementation + library/rec-center gun-ban; voted against Youngkin's transgender-student parental-notification model policy; helped Fairfax earn 'perfect 100' HRC Municipal Equality Index; Equality Act cosponsor (H.R.15, 119th Congress); pushed mail-in/early voting infrastructure; opposes Trump deportation framework and federal concealed-carry reciprocity. Pro-Israel and pro-Ukraine-aid (interventionist). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: (1) explicit no-AIPAC-money pledge — 'I will not accept contributions from AIPAC or any other organization that supports the continuation of the attacks on Gaza' (unusual for a pro-Israel D); (2) China-hawkish trade rhetoric; (3) pro-resourcing (not defund) police-reform framing on Fairfax BOS. Religion: not publicly disclosed.

0.note: Self-described '100% pro-choice'; calls to 'restore and codify Roe v. Wade nationwide' — incompatible with life-from-conception affirmation.

1.note: Led inclusion of 'reproductive freedom' in Fairfax County legislative platform; opposes national bans — opposite of abolition advocacy.

2.note: Priorities page bundles IVF + abortion + prenatal care as reproductive rights — does not oppose embryo discard.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund — listed on official endorsements page.

0.note: Cosponsored Equality Act H.R.15 (119th Congress); HRC-aligned — does not affirm man-woman marriage.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsor — backs SSM codification.

2.note: On Fairfax BOS, voted resolution against Gov. Youngkin's model policy limiting trans-student bathroom access (2022).

4.note: Helped Fairfax County earn 'perfect 100' on HRC Municipal Equality Index — supports LGBTQ promotion in policy.

0.note: Defends Department of Education; pushes federal funding into public-school systems; no school-choice support.

1.note: Voted to OPPOSE Youngkin's parental-notification model policy on transgender students — incompatible with parental-consent affirmation on gender transitions.

2.note: Defends FCPS transgender-protection curriculum direction; voted against Youngkin trans-rollback.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsorship strips religious-employer / RFRA conscience exemptions.

2.note: Equality Act cosponsor + HRC alignment — compelled-speech direction on pronouns/SOGI.
Campaign actively promotes mail-in/absentee/early voting infrastructure — opposes single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Campaign promotes mass mail-in voting access.

0.note: Opposes Trump deportation framework; champions 'comprehensive immigration reform' — opposes border wall + military deployment.

1.note: Publicly opposes ICE detainers; called them an unjudicial burden (Jan 2026); defends Fairfax non-cooperation.

2.note: Authored / pushed the 2021 Fairfax County 'Trust Policy' ending voluntary ICE cooperation — sanctuary-jurisdiction architect. DHS publicly named him a 'Fairfax sanctuary politician' Dec 2025.

0.note: Opposes federal concealed-carry reciprocity overriding state restrictions — opposes constitutional carry.

1.note: Personally championed Fairfax red-flag-law implementation; backs assault-weapon ban + high-capacity-mag restrictions.

2.note: Backs universal background checks and closing the Charleston loophole — opposes NFA/GCA repeal.

4.note: Pro-enforcement gun-control posture; led Fairfax library/rec-center gun-ban ordinance — supports ATF-style regulation.

1.note: Strong Ukraine-aid supporter; framed as carrying on Connolly's pro-Israel, pro-democracy interventionist line — does not support ending forever wars.

2.note: Backs continued U.S. military aid to Israel + Ukraine.

3.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: publicly pledged 'I will not accept contributions from AIPAC or any other organization that supports the continuation of the attacks on Gaza' — unusual for a pro-Israel D and documented refusal of AIPAC money.

profile.religion: Christian — 'active member of his church community' (denomination not publicly disclosed)
profile.background: Virginia House of Delegates, HD-98 (Virginia Beach), since March 17, 2026 (won special election with 62.5% over Cheryl Smith to complete late Del. Barry Knight's term). 15+ year career Deputy Commonwealth's Attorney for Virginia Beach; member Fraternal Order of Police; heads the Virginia Beach Commonwealth's Attorney Gang Task Force. Pungo resident. Endorsed by Rep. Jen Kiggans (VA-2); Youngkin appointee to the Board of the Department of Juvenile Justice prior to candidacy. Committees: Counties/Cities/Towns; Transportation.

andrew-rice.notes_set: VA Delegate HD-98 (R), sworn in March 17, 2026. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] Currency confirmed via WHRO + WAVY + VA House member page H0407. Record is THIN — only ~2.5 months in office, joined the General Assembly after the 2026 regular session was largely complete; no scoreable roll-call record yet. Public_justice cell 0 is HARD TRUE: 15-year prosecutor, FOP member, anti-gang task force lead, campaigned explicitly on backing law enforcement; cells 7.1-7.4 are SOFT-supportive but lack a specific vote/statement. All other cells null per evidence-first rule (no statements located on life, marriage, family, christian liberty, economic stewardship, election integrity, refuse-federal-overreach, border, or self-defense). Religion: generic Christian (denomination not specified). Recommend re-research after the 2027 General Assembly session.
